message:
test(simulation,lifecycle): add shadow-divergence demotion scenario + rustfmt on lifecycle matrix

Two final test landings.

tests/search_asset_simulation.rs:
- `shadow_divergence_demotes_segment_farm_to_verified_serial_path`:
  Sixth scenario in the search-asset simulation. Exercises the
  shadow-mode invariant: when the segment-farm path produces output
  that diverges from the verified serial-replay baseline, the
  rollout gate must demote the segment-farm implementation and
  route live search through the serial path until the divergence is
  investigated.
- Four-beat LoadScript
  (idle:verified_serial_baseline / idle:segment_farm_shadow /
   busy:divergence_review / idle:post_demotion_search).
- Contention plan:
    1. LexicalRepair/record_verified_serial — establish the
       verified-serial output as the ground truth, snapshot hash.
    2. LexicalRepair/compare_shadow_segment_farm — segment-farm
       shadow mode produces a different output hash. Snapshot
       records both hashes + the byte-level divergence count.
    3. LexicalRepair/demote_on_divergence — under the busy
       divergence-review beat, rollout gate demotes segment-farm.
       Snapshot captures the demotion decision (reason =
       "shadow_divergence", pre-demotion traffic share, post-
       demotion serial-only routing).
    4. ForegroundSearch/serve_after_demotion — subsequent searches
       run through the serial path only; assertions check the
       active_path metric = "verified_serial".
- Final assertion set verifies the persisted artifacts carry the
  full divergence evidence (pre/post hashes, byte diff count,
  demotion decision record) so the downstream rollout-gate
  consumer can read back exactly what the harness observed.

tests/lifecycle_matrix.rs:
- Rustfmt reformat on `cass_stdout_json` — the two-argument
  signature splits across lines to fit the 100-col rule. Pure
  formatting; no semantic change.

`.beads/issues.jsonl` + `.beads/last-touched` bump from br sync.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
